title: God in NME
wikitext:
[]: [Spoilers] "God" in NME             Quote Post by DarthGendo » Mon Jun 14, 2010 8:57 am [Originally posted to here , but Darth's post initiated a pretty persistent tangent that seems to be more about gods than Adams, so...] What if SI wasn't related to Adam? What if humans found a unified God on South Pole, like the one we saw in EoE? I don't say it is the exact same, but something like it. And then, it desintegrated into 4 "Adams" in the catastrophic event. It seems that Mark-06 was found on the Moon. It doesn't look like it is a grown clone. What if it is Adam? We saw some opened and closed containers near it. Kaworu came out from one of them. And, as we know from NGE, Adam was found in "White Moon". We see 3 eyes on Eva-01 at the end of RoE2. One of Adams has three eyes. My theory: 4 Adams - Lilith, Eva-01 (maybe still somehow related to Lilith), Mark-06 (Adam), Eva-07 or Eva-08 (related to Adam). There is one problem though. Life on Earth originated from Lilith according to Misato.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Mon Jun 14, 2010 2:04 pm What if SI wasn't related to Adam? What if humans found a unified God on South Pole, like the one we saw in EoE? I don't say it is the exact same, but something like it.

[]:             Quote Post by DarthGendo » Mon Jun 14, 2010 3:24 pm You doubt it based on what? The lack of time? Rebuilds still have to explain those Adams. My theory is just one of possible explainations. Adams=Angels theory has flaws too. Before operation Yashima Gendo says humanity has to destroy 8 more Angels. This means there are 13. 1 - ? (likely Adam) 2 - ? (likely Lilith) 3 - so-called "Tunniel" 4 - Sachiel 5 - Shamshel 6 - Ramiel 7 - so-called "Clockiel" 8 - Sahaquel 9 - Bardiel 10 - Zeruel 11 - ? 12 - ? 13 - ? One of the last three is Tabris, obviously. Could it be that other two are those "Adams"? If not, they're not Angels at all. Because in that case they don't fit in the list. I don't see any clear logic behind Angels' numbering. Are they numbered in order of discovery or in order of actual awakening (coming into action)? If in order of discovery, then Adams should be the first 4 Angels. If in order of awakening, then it is possible that Adam and Lilith are the 1st and the 2nd, and other two are somewhere between 11-13th. But it doesnt' explain, why the "Tunniel" considered 3rd, because he awoke after Sachiel, the 4th Angel.

[]:             Quote Post by Legendary » Mon Jun 14, 2010 3:36 pm No, Tunniel awoke before. They found it BEFORE 1.0 and experimented on it. The incident in 2.0 was just (as far as we can tell) Kaji getting rid of some security measures so that everyone would be distracted while he made his getaway. It's also been hypothesized that the Adams are just Adam split into four fiddly bits because of the experimentations, and thus all of them count as one Angel: the First. It should also be noted that destroying 8 more Angels doesn't necessarily mean that the Adams are part of that contract with Lilith, and that they could qualify as numbers 14, 15, and maybe 16 assuming Lilith and Kaworu aren't one of them.

[]:             Quote Post by DarthGendo » Mon Jun 14, 2010 6:05 pm It is said that the Angel was found in permafrost. Apparently inactive. Otherwise, humans would be unable to stop it. They would keep it frozen for their own safety. Apparently, Kaji sabotaged that security measures. So I have to disagree with you. Tunniel was not awaken until events of RoE2. I have to remind you, nothing was said about the giant of light or Adam particularly. From what we saw in RoE1/2, Adams - are godlike creatures. Eva-01 aquired 3d eye and wings and solely started TI. I think no one will argue about the similarity with one of "Adams". It is straightly said that Eva-01 became equal to god, equal to the NGE Adam in my vision (due to its ability to start the apocalypse). Which means, if Eva-01 is indeed one of Adams, they must be separate creatures with god powers. What can possibly be more powerful than Adam or its equal? Only the unified god we saw in EoE. It's the first thing that comes to my mind. That's my logic. Now another interesting thing. SI in NGE was initiated by humans. Katsuragi worked on S2 theory. He assembled an expedition. In Keel's conversation with Gendo, it is heavily implied that Katsuragi was misled, unaware of consequences of his experiments with the giant of light. He had merged Lance of Longinus with Adam and this has started the catastrophe. SI in Rebuild is different in this aspect too. We have four Lances. I consider this as another argument for the idea of Adams not being parts of one greater Adam.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Mon Jun 14, 2010 6:44 pm It is said that the Angel was found in permafrost. Apparently inactive. Otherwise, humans would be unable to stop it. They would keep it frozen for their own safety. Apparently, Kaji sabotaged that security measures. So I have to disagree with you. Tunniel was not awaken until events of RoE2.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Mon Jun 14, 2010 6:51 pm DarthGendo wrote: It is said that the Angel was found in permafrost. Apparently inactive. Otherwise, humans would be unable to stop it. They would keep it frozen for their own safety. Apparently, Kaji sabotaged that security measures. So I have to disagree with you. Tunniel was not awaken until events of RoE2.

[]:             Quote Post by DarthGendo » Mon Jun 14, 2010 6:54 pm We do? Source me.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Mon Jun 14, 2010 7:06 pm I'm pretty much with AuraTwilight and all his points. Also, DarthGendo, some of your points seem to contradict each other, which may just be a confusion between the movie and series. Double check your work. DarthGendo wrote: I'm not typing in poetry. It's easier to read this way, doesn't it?

[]:             Quote Post by DarthGendo » Mon Jun 14, 2010 7:29 pm Which means no.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Mon Jun 14, 2010 8:44 pm Image of Four Lances

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Mon Jun 14, 2010 9:12 pm AuraTwilight wrote: Ah. Then Presumably it either split with the original Adam entity, theoretically...or it's just a stylistic thing.

[]:             Quote Post by DarthGendo » Tue Jun 15, 2010 3:27 am Your logic sucks.

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Tue Jun 15, 2010 6:10 am DarthGendo wrote: What other godlike beings do we know? Adam of NGE and Lilith of both Rebuild and NGE.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Tue Jun 15, 2010 11:28 am Aura and Darth, knock it off. Aura, you need to stop getting under people's skins; and Darth, you need to take the assholery of Aura or whoever else in stride. Not everyone is going to give your ideas a warm and receptive response, and, if they're jerks about it, not letting things snowball into a fight prevents/minimizes your culpability when the staff pull the mop out. DarthGendo wrote: No, Eva-01 absorbed Eva-00's core.

[]:             Quote Post by DarthGendo » Tue Jun 15, 2010 2:00 pm Azaroth wrote: I don't think either of them qualified as gods initially in NGE.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Jun 16, 2010 11:31 am DarthGendo wrote: Maybe it has essence of Zeruel. It's not important. TI has started long before the absorbtion, right after Shin had said "There's no other Ayanami than you. That's why I'm going to save you".

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Wed Jun 16, 2010 1:48 pm DarthGendo wrote: Traditionally, god is considered to be someone or something immortal. In episode 21 of NGE it is said that SI has occured because a man had tried to become closer to it (read - tried to obtain a fruit of life). As a retribution, the god was lost. Humanity tried to recreate the god. That is Eva. So I think that Lilith and Adam can be considered gods on their own. And it doesn't deny the fact that there can be something even more godly than Adam and Lilith - a supreme being, born through the forbidden unification of Adam and Lilith. Maybe that's the case with Adams. They may be gods, but not the supreme ones. They still can ignore the laws of physics, they can destroy all life on Earth and create a new one given the right condition. For example, Angels are a form of life born from Adam (in NGE). Lilim are a form of life born from Lilith.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Wed Jun 16, 2010 3:44 pm Ornette wrote: There's nothing in rebuild that says there is just 1 Adam. We DO see 4 "Adams" during 2nd Impact.

[]:             Quote Post by DarthGendo » Wed Jun 16, 2010 6:24 pm Sachi Eva-01 looks like one of Adams damn much. Eva-01 is a god in Rebuild that practically initiates TI. If we consider it's due to the absorbtion of Zeruel, due to obtaining of the fruit of life, you have to agree, that SI means this: Adams had both fruits. If each of them had only one fruit, then they had to merge. But this doesn't seem likely. Or maybe those Lances had a missing one they needed. But again, Eva-01 was pierced by the Lance. And it reverted TI. Or the third option: all of them had both fruits. Assuming Eva-01 IS one of original Adams and not a clone or something, why didn't it have both fruits? And if it's a clone, then why when Bardiel merged with it, nothing happened? Too many whys. I'm not convinced. I still think Eva-01 could initiate TI on its own. It just needed a push, a strong motivation of a pilot to awake. If you ask me, why other possible Adams - Lilith, Adam (if it exists) won't repeat what was made 15 years ago, I'll say they're kept inactive, asleep just like Eva-01. Wether it's because they drained all energy after SI, or due to various restraints set up on them (like the Lance in Terminal Dogma and absence of the core in Lilith (scarring)) or magic rituals and spells - I don't know. I agree with most points you made in your post. But for me they apply only to the supreme being (both fruits). And it was Adam they called a god in NGE, not some hypothetical never before seen creature by that time in the series. Anyway, what I was trying to prove in this debate about god is that Adams must be separate beings, not some scattered pieces of one creature, presumably Adam.

Article: God in NME
24 pm Sachi Eva-01 looks like one of Adams damn much. Eva-01 is a god in Rebuild that practically initiates TI. If we consider it's due to the absorbtion of Zeruel, due to obtaining of the fruit of life, you have to agree, that SI means this: Adams had both fruits. If each of them had only one fruit, then they had to merge. But this doesn't seem likely. Or maybe those Lances had a missing one they needed. But again, Eva-01 was pierced by the Lance. And it reverted TI. Or the third option: all of them had both fruits. Assuming Eva-01 IS one of original Adams and not a clone or something, why didn't it have both fruits? And if it's a clone, then why when Bardiel merged with it, nothing happened? Too many whys. I'm not convinced. I still think Eva-01 could initiate TI on its own. It just needed a push, a strong motivation of a pilot to awake. If you ask me, why other possible Adams - Lilith, Adam (if it exists) won't repeat what was made 15 years ago, I'll say they're kept inactive, asleep just like Eva-01. Wether it's because they drained all energy after SI, or due to various restraints set up on them (like the Lance in Terminal Dogma and absence of the core in Lilith (scarring)) or magic rituals and spells - I don't know. I agree with most points you made in your post. But for me they apply only to the supreme being (both fruits). And it was Adam they called a god in NGE, not some hypothetical never before seen creature by that time in the series. Anyway, what I was trying to prove in this debate about god is that Adams must be separate beings, not some scattered pieces of one creature, presumably Adam.
